title: Crispy Moroccan Potatoes Harira
description: A peppery, silky solo-dinner dish that marries crisp potatoes with fresh bonito, simmered in a fragrant tomato broth.
instructions: Wash potatoes and cut into 1 cm cubes.
Heat olive oil in a tagine pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add diced onion and sauté until translucent.
Stir in tomato purée, cumin, coriander, and a pinch of salt; cook for 2 minutes.
Add potato cubes, pour in water, and bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Remove the pot from heat, stir in fresh cilantro and lemon juice.
Sprinkle shaved bonito over the top, allowing it to lightly wilt from the residual heat.
Cover with a lid and let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot straight from the tagine pot.
